valid.name: in-subquery
valid.code: SELECT 1 FROM users WHERE id IN (SELECT user_id FROM admins) LIMIT 100;

valid.name: not-exists
valid.code: SELECT id FROM users u WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM blocks b WHERE b.user_id = u.id) LIMIT 100;

valid.name: not-in-list
valid.code: SELECT 1 FROM users WHERE id NOT IN (1, 2, 3) LIMIT 100;

invalid.name: not-in-subquery
invalid.code: SELECT id FROM users WHERE id NOT IN (SELECT user_id FROM blocks) LIMIT 100;

errors.message: `NOT IN (subquery)` returns no rows if the subquery yields any NULL — almost certainly not what you want. Use `NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM ... WHERE ...)` instead; it handles NULL correctly.
